pattern: Because 'special' is defined purely by whether a 1-indexed position divides the array's length, the whole task reduces to a single pass checking a divisibility condition at each position.
hints: Special positions are entirely about the 1-indexed position number, not the value stored there -- check each position from 1 up to the array's length.
A position i is special exactly when the array's length is evenly divisible by i, which you can test with the modulo operator.
Whenever a position turns out to be special, square the value stored there (remembering the position is 1-indexed) and add it to a running total.

edge_inputs.description: maximum length, maximum values, highly composite length
prime length has only two special positions
highly composite length with all distinct values

stress.description: random arrays across the full allowed size and value range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 50))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 50) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
